# Visual review checklist

Inspect rendered slides directly. Use geometry reports only to locate candidates.

## Whole-slide composition

- Confirm that attention flows naturally from title to major sections to conclusion.
- Check that content is not unintentionally weighted toward one edge or quadrant.
- Confirm that header, footer, page number, and template safe zones remain clear.
- Compare visual density across sections; large empty zones beside compressed content usually indicate a layout opportunity.

## Alignment

- Compare the left and right edges of repeated cards.
- Compare the top and bottom edges of items in the same row.
- Check whether headings, icons, labels, and body text use intentional shared axes.
- Center arrows and connectors relative to the elements they connect.
- Distinguish optical alignment from purely mathematical alignment when icons or rounded shapes look off-center.

## Spacing

- Compare gaps between peer cards and between rows.
- Check title-to-subtitle, header-to-body, and label-to-text spacing.
- Ensure internal padding is sufficient and consistent within repeated containers.
- Provide larger separation between different semantic sections than between items inside one section.
- Avoid elements that merely touch without technically overlapping.

## Typography

- Confirm that titles, section headings, labels, bodies, captions, and footers form a consistent hierarchy.
- Look for words split at unnatural boundaries, one- or two-character orphan fragments, and manual breaks that create ragged short lines.
- Keep related Korean and English terms together when practical.
- Compare peer cards for inconsistent font size, weight, line spacing, and paragraph spacing.
- Judge readability from the rendered size, not only the nominal font size.
- Flag dense cards that require effort to scan even when text technically fits.

## Overlap and containment

- Look for text crossing a fill boundary, rounded corner, icon, divider, or neighboring card.
- Check for objects hidden beneath larger shapes because of z-order.
- Verify that decorative elements do not obscure text.
- Treat intentional containment and background layering as valid when visually coherent.

## Style preservation

- Compare the candidate with the baseline for colors, border radii, strokes, icon style, and visual weight.
- Reject improvements that solve geometry by introducing a new design language.
- Confirm that no text-bearing object has acquired a shadow.
- Confirm that newly created elements look native to the source presentation.

## Final pass

- Review the exact deliverable after copying or renaming it.
- Inspect every affected slide again at full-slide view.
- Reopen or re-query the PPTX and confirm the final preview was rendered from that exact file.
